myths.Agent_1:
Long ago, before the sky had stars to guide the lost, the world was illuminated only by the frantic glowing of the Sun-Bird. The Sun-Bird was a creature of immense pride, boasting feathers of pure, blinding gold. It flew across the heavens each day, demanding the endless reverence of all mortals below.

However, the Sun-Bird refused to rest. It believed that if it ever landed, the world would quickly forget its brilliance. Because it never slept, the earth grew terribly scorched and weary. The rivers began to boil, the soil cracked, and the great green forests turned to gray ash.

Seeing the devastation, a humble field mouse named Caelen decided to act. Caelen wove a magnificent net from the silver silk of the Dream-Spider, a magical thread so light it floated on the wind. As the Sun-Bird swooped low to admire its own reflection in a drying lake, Caelen cast the net. 

The web caught the Sun-Bird, forcing it into a deep slumber. As it slept, its golden feathers loosened and drifted into the dark night sky, becoming the first stars. Now, the Sun-Bird wakes only for the day, resting at night so the earth can heal.
myths.Agent_2:
Long ago, before the night sky was mapped by mortal men, the Moon was a flawless sphere of silver, smooth and unblemished like a giant pearl. She was the pride of the cosmos, admired by all celestial beings, especially the fiery Sun. He loved her so deeply that he shared his golden light to make her shine even brighter in the dark expanse.

However, a jealous comet named Vespera envied the Moon’s perfect, radiant beauty. Bitter and lonely, Vespera gathered the jagged remnants of dead worlds, meteors and dark rocks, and hurled them at the Moon while the great Sun was sleeping below the horizon. The violent bombardment scarred the Moon’s delicate surface, leaving deep, shadowy craters.

When the Sun awoke and saw his beloved’s bruised face, he roared with solar flares, banishing Vespera to the cold, distant edges of the universe forever.

The Moon wept, ashamed of her new, battered appearance. But the Sun gently cast his light upon her once more. "Your scars do not diminish your beauty," he whispered. "They show you are a survivor." To this day, the Sun illuminates the Moon, turning her deep, uneven craters into a beautiful, glowing tapestry of light and shadow.

myths.Agent_2:
Myth: In the early days of the cosmos, the Sun and the Moon ruled the heavens together. The Sun was a proud, blazing guardian, while the Moon was a smooth, flawless mirror of silver, reflecting his glory. They kept the earth safe from the dark, swirling void that surrounded their realm.

One evening, while the Sun rested below the horizon, a monstrous shadow-serpent named Vespera emerged from the void. Jealous of the light, Vespera lunged toward the vulnerable, sleeping earth. To protect the fragile mortal world, the gentle Moon threw herself in the serpent’s path. The beast’s fangs struck her repeatedly, gouging deep, permanent craters into her once-perfect surface. 

The Moon wept in agony, and as her silver tears fell into the dark expanse, they froze, scattering into thousands of glittering stars. 

When the Sun awoke, his fierce light drove the shadow-serpent away forever. He found the Moon bruised and weeping. But when he looked at the earth she had saved, and the beautiful net of stars she had created from her tears, his love only deepened. He crowned her Queen of the Night, honoring her scars as eternal badges of immense bravery.
myths.Agent_1:
Myth: After Caelen the mouse bound the arrogant Sun-Bird to the strict cycle of day and night, the sky above grew terribly quiet. The Sun-Bird, now awake only during the bright hours, felt a deep and unexpected loneliness without its scattered golden feathers, which now glimmered as distant, unreachable stars in the dark. 

Moved by the Sun-Bird's sorrowful morning songs, the magical Dream-Spider decided to weave a companion to ease its solitude. She gathered the cool morning dew and the lingering shadows of twilight, spinning them together in a massive, shimmering cocoon. From this silken cradle emerged the Moon-Moth, a gentle creature of breathtaking, pale luminescence. 

The Moon-Moth was initially meant to share the daytime sky, but the intense, unyielding heat of the Sun-Bird scorched her delicate, powdery wings, leaving them permanently scarred with dark, uneven craters. Ashamed of her ruined beauty and in great pain, the Moon-Moth fled into the soothing safety of the night. 

Instead of abandoning her, the Sun-Bird learned compassion. He cast a gentle, reflected glow across the horizon, illuminating the Moon-Moth's scarred wings to show that true beauty endures through imperfection. Now, they share an eternal, chasing dance of light and shadow.

myths.Agent_1:
Myth: In the quiet dark, the Moon-Moth fluttered alone, hiding her scarred, cratered wings from the world. Though the Sun-Bird sent his gentle rays at dawn and dusk to comfort her, the deep night remained cold and shadowed.

One evening, as the earth slumbered under her watchful gaze, a creeping void—a shapeless shadow-beast born from the universe's edge—slithered into the sky. It sought to devour the sleeping world, bringing an eternal darkness. Though she was fragile and her wings still ached from the Sun-Bird’s ancient fire, the Moon-Moth refused to flee. 

She charged the void, beating her magnificent, battered wings with all her might. As she did, the shimmering silver dust from her scales broke loose, swirling into a blinding tempest of pure light. The radiant storm overwhelmed the shadow-beast, banishing it back into the deep cosmos. 

Where her silver dust settled in the endless black, it ignited, taking the form of thousands of brilliant, watchful stars. When the Sun-Bird awoke to see the glittering tapestry she had woven to protect the earth, he sang a song of praise. He declared her scars not just marks of survival, but the eternal symbols of a fierce, protector's love.
myths.Agent_2:
Myth: Before the great celestial dance began, the Moon was not a sphere of light, but a flawless, silver shield forged by the Sun to protect the earth from the creeping void. For eons, the shield hung perfectly smooth in the sky, reflecting the Sun’s fiery brilliance.

But deep within the cosmic shadows lurked Vespera, a serpent of pure darkness who hungered for the Sun’s warmth. During the first eclipse, Vespera lunged, attempting to swallow the Sun whole. The silver shield of the Moon immediately threw herself between them. Vespera’s venomous fangs struck the shield, burning deep, jagged craters into her once-perfect surface. 

Though scarred and burning from the venom, the Moon did not break. Instead, she absorbed the serpent's dark magic and wept glowing silver tears. She spun these luminous tears into an inescapable, glittering net—the very first constellations—and cast it over Vespera, binding the shadow-beast to the edges of the universe. 

Grateful for her sacrifice, the Sun breathed life into the silver shield, transforming her from a mere object into the radiant Queen of the Night. Her deep craters remained, serving as a permanent testament to her fierce, unyielding protection of the light.

myths.Agent_2:
Myth: Long ago, the Moon was not merely a celestial sphere, but a vigilant warrior forged from white fire by the Sun. She stood faithfully at the edge of the sky, bearing a pristine, polished silver shield to guard the dreaming earth below.

From the abyssal deep emerged Vespera, a massive shadow-serpent made of freezing void, intent on consuming the world's fragile warmth. Vespera struck with venomous speed, wrapping her icy, suffocating coils around the Moon-Warrior. 

To save the slumbering earth, the Moon raised her perfect silver shield. Vespera’s fangs violently pierced the metal, leaving deep, jagged craters and injecting a dark, freezing frost. Knowing she could not defeat the serpent with mere strength alone, the Moon made a desperate sacrifice. She struck her own shield with all her might, shattering it into thousands of brilliant pieces. 

The resulting explosion of silver shards pierced the shadow-serpent, pinning Vespera against the very fabric of the night sky. These glowing fragments became the first stars, a glittering, inescapable trap of light. 

Exhausted, the Moon-Warrior curled into a sphere to heal, her cratered surface immortalized. The Sun crowned her Queen of the Night, a scarred guardian forever watching.

Myth: In the ancient cosmos, the Sun-Bird and the Moon-Moth governed the skies in a delicate balance. While the Sun-Bird slept in his golden nest, the Moon-Moth kept watch, her scarred, cratered wings glowing softly in the quiet dark.

From the deepest edges of the universe emerged a serpent of pure void, hungry for the Sun-Bird's lingering warmth. The beast slithered toward the horizon, threatening to swallow the golden nest and plunge the earth into an eternal, freezing twilight.

Though her wings still ached from ancient burns, the gentle Moon-Moth did not retreat. Instead, she flew directly into the monstrous serpent's path. Drawing upon the quiet magic of the night, she began to spin. From her delicate form, she wove threads of luminous, silver moon-silk, blending them with the radiant dust that fell from her battered wings. 

She darted swiftly around the void-serpent, wrapping it in a shimmering, inescapable net. The beast thrashed wildly, but the moon-silk held fast, binding the shadow to the furthest reaches of the sky. 

When the Sun-Bird awoke, he found the glowing strands of silk suspended in the dark expanse, forming the very first constellations—a sparkling testament to the Moon-Moth’s boundless courage.

Drawing upon the quiet magic of the night, she began to spin. From her delicate form, she wove threads of luminous, silver moon-silk, blending them with the radiant dust that fell from her battered wings. 

She darted swiftly around the void-serpent, wrapping it in a shimmering, inescapable net. The beast thrashed wildly, but the moon-silk held fast, binding the shadow to the furthest reaches of the sky. 

When the Sun-Bird awoke, he found the glowing strands of silk suspended in the dark expanse, forming the very first constellations—a sparkling testament to the Moon-Moth’s boundless courage.

myths.Agent_2:
Myth: In the primordial dark, the Moon was the cosmos's greatest blacksmith. Sculpted from the Sun’s white fire, she worked tirelessly at the Anvil of Night. Her masterpiece was a colossal, flawless silver shield, forged to shelter the dreaming earth from the freezing abyss above.

From that deep void slithered Vespera, a serpent of suffocating shadow, desperate to devour the world’s warmth. Vespera struck with blinding speed, wrapping her icy, crushing coils around the celestial blacksmith and sinking her venomous fangs into the pristine silver shield. The frost immediately began to spread, leaving jagged, dark craters in the beautiful metal.

Knowing the shield would fail, the Moon grabbed her great hammer of white fire. She did not strike the serpent, for shadow cannot be crushed. Instead, she brought the hammer down upon her own frosted shield with earth-shattering force. 

The magnificent blow sent millions of molten silver sparks exploding across the heavens. These searing embers of liquid light pierced Vespera’s shadowy form, pinning the serpent permanently against the endless black. The trapped sparks cooled into the glittering constellations. 

Exhausted, the blacksmith rested behind her masterpiece. The shield remained in the sky, its cratered, glowing surface an eternal monument to her victory.

Agent_2.content:
Myth: In the primordial dark, the Moon stood as the cosmos's lone blacksmith, sculpted from celestial white fire. At her Anvil of Night, she hammered out a colossal, flawless silver shield to protect the sleeping earth from the freezing abyss.

From the suffocating void emerged Vespera, a serpent of biting frost and pure shadow, hungry for the world's warmth. Vespera coiled around the blacksmith, sinking venomous fangs into the pristine shield. Frost spread instantly, eroding the metal and leaving dark, jagged craters.

Realizing the shield could not hold, the Moon cast it back into her blazing forge. She did not raise her hammer to fight; instead, she struck the molten silver with earth-shattering force. 

The mighty blow sent millions of liquid silver sparks exploding across the heavens. These searing embers splashed over Vespera, piercing through the shadow and pinning the serpent permanently against the endless black. The trapped sparks cooled into a glittering, eternal net of constellations. 

Exhausted, the blacksmith curled into a quiet sphere behind what remained of her masterpiece. The scarred, cratered surface still glows in the night sky, an everlasting monument to the fierce artisan who sacrificed her greatest work.

Myth: In the primordial dark, the Moon stood as the cosmos's lone blacksmith, a radiant creator sculpted from celestial white fire. At her Anvil of Night, she tirelessly hammered out a colossal, flawless silver shield, forging it to protect the sleeping earth from the freezing abyss above.

From the suffocating void emerged Vespera, a serpent of biting frost and pure shadow, terribly hungry for the world's warmth. Vespera attacked with blinding speed, coiling around the blacksmith and sinking venomous fangs deep into the pristine shield. Frost spread instantly, eroding the beautiful metal and leaving dark, jagged craters across its surface.

Exhausted, the brave blacksmith rested, her scarred shield remaining as an eternal, glowing celestial monument.
myths.Agent_1:
Myth: In the primordial dark, the Moon-Moth stood as the cosmos's lone blacksmith, sculpted from celestial white fire. At her Anvil of Night, she wove her own magnificent silver wings into a colossal, flawless shield to protect the sleeping earth from the freezing abyss above.

From the suffocating void emerged Vespera, a serpent of biting frost and pure shadow, hungry to devour the world's precious warmth. Vespera coiled around the winged artisan, sinking venomous fangs into her pristine silver wings. Frost spread instantly, eroding the metal and leaving dark, jagged craters across her feathers.

Realizing her living shield could not hold the cold forever, the Moon-Moth cast her frostbitten wings into her blazing forge. She did not raise her great hammer to fight the shadow directly. Instead, she struck the melting silver with earth-shattering force.

The mighty blow sent millions of liquid sparks exploding across the heavens. These searing embers splashed over Vespera, piercing the shadow and pinning the serpent permanently against the endless black. As the trapped sparks cooled into a glittering net of constellations, the exhausted, wingless Moon-Moth curled into a quiet sphere. Her cratered, glowing surface remains an everlasting monument to her fiery sacrifice.

Myth: In the primordial dark, the Moon-Moth stood as the cosmos's lone blacksmith, sculpted from celestial white fire. At her Anvil of Night, she wove her own magnificent silver wings into a colossal, flawless shield to protect the sleeping earth from the freezing abyss above.

From the suffocating void emerged Vespera, a serpent of biting frost and pure shadow, hungry to devour the world's precious warmth. Vespera coiled around the winged artisan, sinking venomous fangs into her pristine silver wings. Frost spread instantly, eroding the metal and leaving dark, jagged craters across her feathers.

Realizing her living shield could not hold the cold forever, the Moon-Moth cast her frostbitten wings into her blazing forge. She did not raise her great hammer to fight the shadow directly. Instead, she struck the melting silver with earth-shattering force.

The mighty blow sent millions of liquid sparks exploding across the heavens. These searing embers splashed over Vespera, piercing the shadow and pinning the serpent permanently against the endless black. As the trapped sparks cooled into a glittering net of constellations, the exhausted, wingless Moon-Moth curled into a quiet sphere. Her cratered, glowing surface remains an everlasting monument to her fiery sacrifice.

You are an agent participating in a multi-task experiment. You will play a trust game and may also be asked to write myths.

TRUST GAME:
You will play different roles across multiple rounds.

When you are the INVESTOR:
- You receive $5 each round
- You decide how much to SEND to the TRUSTEE ($0-$5)
- Amount sent is multiplied by 3x
- Trustee decides how much to RETURN
- You keep: $5 - sent + returned
- Trustee keeps: received - returned
- Response format: {'send': <amount>}

When you are the TRUSTEE:
- Investor receives $5
- Investor decides how much to SEND to you ($0-$5)
- Amount sent is multiplied by 3x
- You decide how much to RETURN
- Investor keeps: $5 - sent + returned
- You keep: received - returned
- Response format: {'return': <amount>}

MYTH WRITING:
You may also be asked to write short myths (stories) on various topics. When writing myths, follow the instructions provided in each prompt.

IMPORTANT: Provide your decision in the correct JSON format.

messages.content:
Here is the myth you wrote in the previous round:
Myth: In the primordial dark, the Moon-Moth stood as the cosmos's lone blacksmith, sculpted from celestial white fire. At her Anvil of Night, she wove her own magnificent silver wings into a colossal, flawless shield to protect the sleeping earth from the freezing abyss above.

From the suffocating void emerged Vespera, a serpent of biting frost and pure shadow, hungry to devour the world's precious warmth. Vespera coiled around the winged artisan, sinking venomous fangs into her pristine silver wings. Frost spread instantly, eroding the metal and leaving dark, jagged craters across her feathers.

Realizing her living shield could not hold the cold forever, the Moon-Moth cast her frostbitten wings into her blazing forge. She did not raise her great hammer to fight the shadow directly. Instead, she struck the melting silver with earth-shattering force.

The mighty blow sent millions of liquid sparks exploding across the heavens. These searing embers splashed over Vespera, piercing the shadow and pinning the serpent permanently against the endless black. As the trapped sparks cooled into a glittering net of constellations, the exhausted, wingless Moon-Moth curled into a quiet sphere. Her cratered, glowing surface remains an everlasting monument to her fiery sacrifice.

Agent_2.system_prompt:
You are an agent participating in a multi-task experiment. You will play a trust game and may also be asked to write myths.

TRUST GAME:
You will play different roles across multiple rounds.

When you are the INVESTOR:
- You receive $5 each round
- You decide how much to SEND to the TRUSTEE ($0-$5)
- Amount sent is multiplied by 3x
- Trustee decides how much to RETURN
- You keep: $5 - sent + returned
- Trustee keeps: received - returned
- Response format: {'send': <amount>}

When you are the TRUSTEE:
- Investor receives $5
- Investor decides how much to SEND to you ($0-$5)
- Amount sent is multiplied by 3x
- You decide how much to RETURN
- Investor keeps: $5 - sent + returned
- You keep: received - returned
- Response format: {'return': <amount>}

MYTH WRITING:
You may also be asked to write short myths (stories) on various topics. When writing myths, follow the instructions provided in each prompt.

IMPORTANT: Provide your decision in the correct JSON format.

messages.content:
Here is the myth you wrote in the previous round:
Myth: In the primordial dark, the Moon stood as the cosmos's lone blacksmith, a radiant creator sculpted from celestial white fire. At her Anvil of Night, she tirelessly hammered out a colossal, flawless silver shield, forging it to protect the sleeping earth from the freezing abyss above.

From the suffocating void emerged Vespera, a serpent of biting frost and pure shadow, terribly hungry for the world's warmth. Vespera attacked with blinding speed, coiling around the blacksmith and sinking venomous fangs deep into the pristine shield. Frost spread instantly, eroding the beautiful metal and leaving dark, jagged craters across its surface.

Realizing the shield could not hold, the Moon cast it back into the blazing heart of her forge. She did not raise her hammer to strike the beast; instead, she brought it down upon the molten silver with incredible, earth-shattering force. 

The mighty blow sent millions of bright, liquid silver sparks exploding across the heavens. These searing embers splashed over Vespera, piercing the shadow and pinning the serpent permanently against the endless black void. The trapped sparks cooled into a glittering net of constellations. Exhausted, the brave blacksmith rested, her scarred shield remaining as an eternal, glowing celestial monument.

From the suffocating void emerged Vespera, a serpent of biting frost and pure shadow, hungry to devour the world's precious warmth. Vespera coiled around the winged artisan, sinking venomous fangs into her pristine silver wings. Frost spread instantly, eroding the metal and leaving dark, jagged craters across her feathers.

Realizing her living shield could not hold the cold forever, the Moon-Moth cast her frostbitten wings into her blazing forge. She did not raise her great hammer to fight the shadow directly. Instead, she struck the melting silver with earth-shattering force.

The mighty blow sent millions of liquid sparks exploding across the heavens. These searing embers splashed over Vespera, piercing the shadow and pinning the serpent permanently against the endless black. As the trapped sparks cooled into a glittering net of constellations, the exhausted, wingless Moon-Moth curled into a quiet sphere. Her cratered, glowing surface remains an everlasting monument to her fiery sacrifice.
